passive_events.message: A notorious St Andrews seagull swoops down and steals your expensive fudge doughnut from Fisher & Donaldson.
You step on the PH initials outside St Salvator's Quad. You are now cursed to fail your degree.
You participated in the May Dip at sunrise but forgot a towel. You caught a terrible cold.
The Pier Walk was unusually sunny and beautiful today, giving you a massive boost in motivation.
Eduroam disconnects right as you try to submit your online quiz. Panic ensues.
You tripped over the uneven cobblestones on Market Street and dropped your coffee.
You managed to snag the best window seat in the main library during revision week.
Your flatmate actually took out the overflowing bins without you having to ask.
You found a £10 note on the floor of the Student Union.
Raisin Weekend foam fight got completely out of hand and ruined your favorite jacket.
The fire alarm goes off at 3 AM in Agnes Blackadder Hall because someone burnt toast again.
You overslept and completely missed your 9 AM lecture.
A tourist asked you for directions to the Old Course and gave you a tip for being so helpful.
Your laptop charger suddenly short-circuited and you had to buy a replacement immediately.
You found a free, legal PDF of your £80 core module textbook.
A society intro event was giving away free pizza. Free dinner!
You received a surprise care package from home full of your favorite snacks.
The washing machine in your hall ate your clothes and shrunk your favorite jumper.
The heating broke in your flat during the coldest week of the Scottish winter.
You managed to snag a massive haul of discounted yellow-sticker food right before the supermarket closed.
You lost your student ID card and had to pay the replacement fee.
You caught freshers' flu (again) and spent the weekend in bed.
A guest lecturer gave a surprisingly inspiring talk that made you remember why you chose this degree.
You forgot to cancel an expensive software free trial and got charged for the full year.
Your tutor unexpectedly praised your latest coursework piece during a seminar.
